actionable_feedback: Include a machine-readable calculation_log.csv (or .txt) that contains the raw arithmetic and row-by-row computations used to derive every reported metric. Each row should state the formula, the operand values (with packet citations), and the computed result.

Add a decision_table.csv that maps input counts and thresholds to the recommended action (accept/reject) and documents the cost-weighted decision rule. Include columns: TP, FP, TN, FN, defect_prior (if used), threshold_p_defect, decision, weighted_cost_components.

Fix the confusion-matrix labeling in porosity_QC_report.md so the table labels (TN, FP, FN, TP) align with the formulas and the packet citations. Add an explicit legend linking each table cell to the variable names used in the formulas (e.g., FP = true_defect predicted_good).

Provide a small numeric sensitivity analysis. At minimum, show how +/-5% changes to the defect counts or to the estimated defect probability would change the weighted_misclassification_cost and whether the recommended cost-threshold (p < 0.1667) still holds. Include the numeric delta and a short interpretation.

If available, attach model calibration artifacts (score-to-probability mapping), a threshold sweep (ROC/PR or threshold grid), or explain explicitly that such artifacts are unavailable and why. If not available, mark the model-score cutoff as needs_review and document how a calibrated cutoff should be computed.

Ensure all newly added files are referenced in the report with exact filenames and locations, and update ActualOutputs to include these files so the deterministic artifact-contract precheck will pass for the full deliverable set.

feedback_summary: The submitted porosity_QC_report.md contains correct numeric calculations (precision, recall, defect-escape/false-accept rate, and cost-threshold p < 1/6) and clear validation checks, but it is missing required machine-readable artifacts (calculation_log.csv and decision_table.csv), contains a confusion-matrix label inconsistency (FP/FN labels swapped in the table vs. the formulas), and lacks a quantified sensitivity analysis. These issues justify partial credit despite the arithmetic being auditable and correct.

extra_model_fields.expected_effect_of_revision: Include missing machine-readable artifacts and fix labels should raise semantic score to full credit; adding sensitivity analysis improves auditability.
